deep_dive.title: Decentralized Gradient Descent: Bottleneck Regimes and Budget Complexity
deep_dive.abstract: Decentralized gradient descent (DGD) is widely used for solving distributed optimization problems over networks of agents. While its convergence properties are well understood, less is known about the communication and computation resources required to attain a prescribed accuracy. In this paper, we study DGD from a resource-aware perspective and characterize the communication-computation budget required to attain a target error level. We develop a bottleneck-centric framework in which different factors dominate the optimization dynamics at different error scales. Specifically, we identify operating regimes governed by initialization, objective heterogeneity and network connectivity, gradient noise, and communication noise. To capture these effects, we introduce two fundamental quantities: the gradient-Diversity-to-Network-connectivity Ratio (DNR) and the Gradient-to-Communication-noise Ratio (GCR). We show that these quantities determine the sequence of bottlenecks encountered during optimization and the corresponding budget-optimal operating strategy. Using a multi-stage analysis, we derive optimal stepsize selections and explicit budget-complexity bounds that quantify the budget resources required to attain a prescribed accuracy. The resulting expressions reveal how the overall budget decomposes into contributions associated with successive bottlenecks and provide insight into the fundamental tradeoffs among objective heterogeneity, network connectivity, gradient noise, and communication noise.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: communication-computation budget analysis for decentralized gradient descent
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 分布式梯度下降中的通信-计算预算分析
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 分布式梯度下降中的通信-计算预算分析
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 分布式梯度下降中的通信-计算预算分析
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper characterizes the communication and computation budgets required for decentralized gradient descent, identifying bottleneck regimes and providing a resource-aware framework.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对分布式梯度下降算法，从资源感知角度出发，系统分析了在不同误差尺度下主导优化动力学的瓶颈因素，包括初始化、目标异质性、网络连通性、梯度噪声和通信噪声等，刻画了达到目标精度所需的通信-计算预算，为设计通信高效的分布式学习系统提供了理论依据和预算指导。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文针对分布式梯度下降算法，从资源感知角度出发，系统分析了在不同误差尺度下主导优化动力学的瓶颈因素，包括初始化、目标异质性、网络连通性、梯度噪声和通信噪声等，刻画了达到目标精度所需的通信-计算预算，为设计通信高效的分布式学习系统提供了理论依据和预算指导。

quick_skim.title: Robust Decentralized Optimization under Node Failures via Adaptive Regularization
quick_skim.abstract: We study decentralized minimization of a sum of functions over a network where nodes may only leave, the remaining nodes stay connected, and the topology freezes between departures. Standard methods forget departed functions, causing a permanent bias proportional to the heterogeneity of the data. We propose Legacy Gradient Tracking (Legacy-GT): before leaving, a node compresses its function into a gradient-anchored quadratic legacy, bequeaths it to a neighbor, and provides a correction that exactly preserves the gradient-tracking invariant. We prove that the optimal legacy curvature is the average of the strong-convexity and smoothness constants, that legacies compose losslessly across chained departures, and that an adaptive anchor rule yields error bounds that decay geometrically after the last departure to a small residual--the minimum of the network's optimization error at departure and its heterogeneity radius. In contrast, the classic drop-and-forget baseline suffers a bias that never decays. Numerical experiments confirm the theory.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: decentralized optimization with legacy compression for communication efficiency
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过遗留压缩实现通信高效的分布式优化
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过遗留压缩实现通信高效的分布式优化
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过遗留压缩实现通信高效的分布式优化
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Legacy-GT that compresses node functions into quadratic legacies to reduce communication in decentralized optimization under node failures.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对节点故障下的分布式优化，提出Legacy-GT方法，节点离开前将其函数压缩为梯度锚定的二次遗留项传递给邻居，精确保持梯度追踪不变性，从而消除永久偏差并减少通信开销。理论证明最优遗留曲率等于强凸性与光滑性常数的平均值，并支持链式节点离开的损失less组合。该方法为分布式训练中的通信高效提供了新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对节点故障下的分布式优化，提出Legacy-GT方法，节点离开前将其函数压缩为梯度锚定的二次遗留项传递给邻居，精确保持梯度追踪不变性，从而消除永久偏差并减少通信开销。理论证明最优遗留曲率等于强凸性与光滑性常数的平均值，并支持链式节点离开的损失less组合。该方法为分布式训练中的通信高效提供了新思路。
